title: Fix handling of /dev/null masked devices
body:
If a masked object is already a /dev/null device then don't mask over it.

This logic is backwords and is breaking SELinux.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: LGTM.  Can we add a test for this?
@nalind Not sure how to create tests.
I hit up the ubuntu test once already and it failed, will try again.
@TomSweeneyRedHat Once travis passes I will merge.

LGTM
if you need to repush, please touch up the comment, otherwise later is fine.
